subject: eddy-lazzarin

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge proofs & zkEVMs
sectors_excited.why: 2022 turned ZK from a research topic into shipping infrastructure — multiple EVM-compatible zk-rollups reached testnet/mainnet this year. ZK is the most important primitive in crypto right now: scalability via validity proofs plus a real path to privacy and verifiable computation. This is where I'd want the strongest technical teams concentrating.

sectors_excited.name: Ethereum L2 rollups & the rollup-centric roadmap
sectors_excited.why: Post-Merge, Ethereum's scaling story runs through rollups. Optimistic and ZK rollups are absorbing real usage at a fraction of L1 cost, and data-availability improvements on the horizon (proto-danksharding/EIP-4844) make the unit economics dramatically better. The base layer becomes settlement + DA; execution moves up the stack.

sectors_excited.name: Account abstraction & smart-contract wallets
sectors_excited.why: ERC-4337 lets us rethink key management, social recovery, gas sponsorship, and session keys without touching consensus. After a year where custodial failures wiped people out, self-custody with humane UX is the single biggest unlock for mainstream usability. Underrated heading into 2023.

sectors_excited.name: Modular blockchain architecture & data availability
sectors_excited.why: Separating execution, settlement, consensus, and data availability into specialized layers is proving to be the right factoring. Dedicated DA and modular designs let teams optimize each layer independently — I think this architecture beats monolithic chains for general-purpose scaling.

sectors_excited.name: On-chain identity, reputation & NFTs as ownership primitives
sectors_excited.why: NFTs as a general ownership primitive — not just art — plus early on-chain identity and non-transferable/reputation work point to durable, non-speculative use cases. These are the building blocks for on-chain social and credentialing that don't depend on token price to be useful.

sectors_concerned.name: Centralized exchanges & opaque CeFi
sectors_concerned.why: FTX's collapse this quarter was fraud and a custody failure, not a technology failure — but it exposed how much counterparty and commingling risk sits inside centralized intermediaries. Until there is real proof-of-reserves/proof-of-liabilities and genuine segregation of customer assets, I'm deeply wary of opaque CeFi. Not your keys, not your coins.

sectors_concerned.name: Unsustainable yield & reflexive tokenomics
sectors_concerned.why: Terra/UST, Celsius, and the leverage chain that took down 3AC shared one disease: yields and valuations propped up by reflexive token emissions and circular leverage rather than real economic activity. I'm skeptical of anything promising high 'risk-free' yield and of designs that depend on perpetual new inflows.

sectors_concerned.name: US regulatory overhang & ongoing contagion
sectors_concerned.why: Post-FTX, expect aggressive enforcement and political pressure. The near-term danger is heavy-handed, builder-hostile rules that push legitimate teams offshore, alongside contagion still working through lenders and intermediaries into early 2023. My worry is legitimate builders getting caught in the backlash from others' fraud.

tokens_excited.name: ETH
tokens_excited.why: Not a price call. Post-Merge, Ethereum is proof-of-stake with sharply lower issuance and a credible rollup-centric scaling path. As the settlement and data-availability layer for the L2 ecosystem and the dominant smart-contract platform, it's the asset whose technical trajectory I have the most conviction in — the network shipped what it promised this year.

risk_regime.why: Near-term and for liquid markets I'm defensive: FTX just failed, Genesis/DCG contagion is still unwinding, and macro liquidity is tight. Don't touch leverage or opaque counterparties right now. But this is a liquid-market posture, not a statement about the technology — as a long-term venture investor I'm constructively deploying into builders through the bear, which is exactly when the best teams build heads-down.

notes: My logged personal record here is empty, so every item is honestly marked 'extrapolated' — inferred from how I think as a16z crypto's CTO at the end of 2022, not from verbatim prior statements; no quotes are fabricated. I express conviction at the technology/sector level rather than trading specific tokens, which is why tokens_concerned is empty (my CeFi and tokenomics worries are captured as sector concerns). Prices have reset hard this year, but the 2022 technical milestones — the Merge, ZK going to production — make me more excited about the foundations for the next cycle than the price action would suggest.
